Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Datahäxan 116 upvotes, 9 comments
  2. Printf-Tac-Toe 99 upvotes, 9 comments
  3. Dolphin Progress Release 2603 220 upvotes, 34 comments
  4. Long Overlooked as Crucial to Life, Fungi Start to Get Their Due 14 upvotes, 0 comments
  5. High fidelity font synthesis for CJK languages 39 upvotes, 4 comments
  6. Italian prosecutors seek trial for Amazon, 4 execs in alleged $1.4B tax evasion 87 upvotes, 15 comments
  7. Avoiding Trigonometry (2013) 175 upvotes, 46 comments
  8. The Met Releases High-Def 3D Scans of 140 Famous Art Objects 73 upvotes, 17 comments
  9. Malus – Clean Room as a Service 466 upvotes, 163 comments
  10. Tested: How Many Times Can a DVD±RW Be Rewritten? Methodology and Results 225 upvotes, 75 comments
  11. 3D-Knitting: The Ultimate Guide 188 upvotes, 66 comments
  12. Reversing memory loss via gut-brain communication 8 upvotes, 0 comments
  13. Reliable Software in the LLM Era 79 upvotes, 26 comments
  14. The Cost of Indirection in Rust 33 upvotes, 8 comments
  15. Show HN: LogClaw – Open-source AI SRE that auto-creates tickets from logs 6 upvotes, 0 comments
  16. Full Spectrum and Infrared Photography 6 upvotes, 0 comments
  17. Emacs internals: Tagged pointers vs. C++ std:variant and LLVM (Part 3) 43 upvotes, 17 comments
  18. Asia rolls out 4-day weeks, WFH to solve fuel crisis caused by Iran war 180 upvotes, 98 comments
  19. US banks' exposure to private credit hits $300B (2025) 167 upvotes, 97 comments
  20. Returning to Rails in 2026 305 upvotes, 190 comments
  21. Claude now creates interactive charts, diagrams and visualizations 51 upvotes, 24 comments
  22. Show HN: Understudy – Teach a desktop agent by demonstrating a task once 5 upvotes, 0 comments
  23. Show HN: We analyzed 1,573 Claude Code sessions to see how AI agents work 99 upvotes, 63 comments
  24. Show HN: OneCLI – Vault for AI Agents in Rust 19 upvotes, 9 comments
  25. Kotlin creator's new language: a formal way to talk to LLMs instead of English 149 upvotes, 123 comments
  26. Big Data on the Cheapest MacBook 244 upvotes, 214 comments
  27. Bubble Sorted Amen Break 6 upvotes, 1 comments
  28. Suburban school district uses license plate readers to verify student residency 140 upvotes, 165 comments
  29. ATMs didn't kill bank Teller jobs, but the iPhone did 132 upvotes, 164 comments
